attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a rinsed cloth in a cabinet.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a bathroom or utility area centered around a sink vanity. The primary objects relevant to the domain and the instruction are as follows:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Receptacles:
     - Sink: A beige, oval-shaped basin embedded in a grey countertop. It is a receptacle as it can hold objects (e.g., for cleaning). It currently contains a pink bar of soap.
     - Cabinet: A white, double-door cabinet located directly beneath the sink. It is a receptacle as it can store items. The cabinet doors are closed.
     - Blue Bin: A blue plastic trash bin positioned to the right of the cabinet. It is a receptacle, though not directly relevant to the current task.
   - Objects:
     - Cloth: A red, crumpled cloth resting on the grey countertop to the left of the sink. It is an object and is the target for rinsing and storing.
     - Spray Bottle: A purple spray bottle standing upright on the countertop, to the left of the sink. It is an object but not relevant to the task.
     - Candle: A small, light green cylindrical candle on the countertop, next to the cloth. It is an object but not relevant to the task.
     - Soap: A pink rectangular bar of soap inside the sink basin. It is an object.

2. Quantities of each object type:
   - Receptacles: 3 (Sink, Cabinet, Blue Bin)
   - Objects: 4 (Cloth, Spray Bottle, Candle, Soap)

3. Spatial relationships (matching domain predicates):
   - The cloth is on the countertop, to the left of the sink.
   - The soap is in the sink (inReceptacle soap sink).
   - The cabinet is directly under the sink.
   - The blue bin is to the right of the cabinet.
   - The spray bottle and candle are on the countertop, adjacent to the cloth.
   - The faucet is above the sink, but it is not defined as an object or receptacle in the domain.

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - The cloth is not clean (not isClean cloth) and is not being held by any agent.
   - The sink is not currently being used for cleaning (no agent is holding the cloth over it).
   - The cabinet is closed (not opened cabinet) and contains no visible items (not inReceptacle any object cabinet).
   - The agent is not at any location (not atLocation agent any object).

5. Colors, positions, and distinguishing features:
   - The countertop is a solid grey surface.
   - The sink is beige with a smooth, oval basin.
   - The cabinet is white with two doors, each featuring a small, vertical, metallic handle.
   - The cloth is red and appears crumpled, placed near the left edge of the countertop.
   - The spray bottle is purple with a white nozzle.
   - The candle is light green and cylindrical.
   - The soap is pink and rectangular, resting at the bottom of the sink.
   - The blue bin is a tall, rectangular plastic container with a slightly tapered top.
   - The floor is a grey marble-patterned tile.

The task requires the agent to pick up the cloth, clean it in the sink, and then place it inside the cabinet. The cabinet must be opened first, and the cloth must be held by the agent during cleaning. The current state indicates that none of these actions have been initiated.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_rinsed_cloth_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent - agent
        sink - sink
        cabinet - receptacle
        cloth - object
        soap - object
        blue_bin - receptacle
        spray_bottle - object
        candle - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (inReceptacle soap sink)
        (not (isClean cloth))
        (not (holdsAny agent))
        (not (atLocation agent sink))
        (not (atLocation agent cabinet))
        (not (atLocation agent cloth))
        (atLocation agent ?o) ; This is a placeholder; the agent is not at any specific location initially.
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle cloth cabinet)
        (isClean cloth)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_rinsed_cloth_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent - agent
        sink - sink
        cabinet - receptacle
        cloth - object
        soap - object
        blue_bin - receptacle
        spray_bottle - object
        candle - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable cabinet)
        (not (opened cabinet))
        (inReceptacle soap sink)
        (not (isClean cloth))
        (not (holdsAny agent))
        (not (atLocation agent sink))
        (not (atLocation agent cabinet))
        (not (atLocation agent cloth))
        (atLocation agent ?o) ; This is a placeholder; the agent is not at any specific location initially.
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle cloth cabinet)
        (isClean cloth)
    ))
)

    Instruction: Put a rinsed cloth in a cabinet.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Agent: A robotic agent (not visually depicted but assumed to be present for task execution).
   - Receptacles:
     - Sink: A beige oval-shaped sink basin with a dark faucet, embedded in a gray countertop. It is a receptacle capable of holding objects for cleaning.
     - Cabinet: A light gray under-sink cabinet with two doors, each having a small vertical handle. It is a receptacle capable of storing objects. The cabinet is closed.
   - Objects:
     - Cloth: A red, crumpled cloth resting on the countertop to the left of the sink. It is not yet cleaned.
     - Candle: A small, light green cylindrical candle on the countertop, to the left of the cloth. Not relevant to the task.
     - Spray Bottle: A tall, purple spray bottle standing upright on the countertop to the right of the cloth. Not relevant to the task.
     - Soap: A small, pink rectangular bar of soap resting inside the sink basin. Not relevant to the task.
     - Trash Bin: A blue rectangular trash bin positioned to the right of the cabinet, partially visible. Not a receptacle in the domain definition.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 sink (receptacle)
   - 1 cabinet (receptacle)
   - 1 cloth (object)
   - 1 candle (object)
   - 1 spray bottle (object)
   - 1 soap bar (object)
   - 1 trash bin (object, not a receptacle per domain)

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The cloth is on the countertop, to the left of the sink.
   - The sink is centrally located on the countertop.
   - The cabinet is directly beneath the sink and countertop.
   - The candle and spray bottle are on the countertop, with the candle to the left of the cloth and the spray bottle to the right of the cloth.
   - The soap is inside the sink basin.
   - The trash bin is on the floor to the right of the cabinet.
   - The agent is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:
   - The cloth is not cleaned (not isClean).
   - The cabinet is closed (not opened).
   - The sink is available for use (no object currently being cleaned in it).
   - The agent does not hold any object (not holdsAny).

5. Visual Properties:
   - The countertop is dark gray and smooth.
   - The sink is beige with a dark metallic faucet.
   - The cabinet is light gray with two doors and small vertical handles.
   - The cloth is red and crumpled.
   - The candle is light green and cylindrical.
   - The spray bottle is purple and tall.
   - The soap is pink and rectangular.
   - The trash bin is blue and rectangular.
   - The floor is gray with a marbled texture.

The task requires the agent to first go to the cloth, pick it up, then go to the sink, clean the cloth, then go to the cabinet, open it, and put the rinsed cloth inside. The cabinet must be opened before placing the cloth inside.

attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_rinsed_cloth_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        cloth1 - object
        candle1 - object
        spray_bottle1 - object
        soap1 - object
        trash_bin1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 cloth1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean cloth1))
        (not (opened cabinet1))
        (openable cabinet1)
        (inReceptacle soap1 sink1)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle cloth1 cabinet1)
        (isClean cloth1)
        (opened cabinet1)
    ))
)
```

(define (problem put_rinsed_cloth_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        cloth1 - object
        candle1 - object
        spray_bottle1 - object
        soap1 - object
        trash_bin1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 cloth1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean cloth1))
        (not (opened cabinet1))
        (openable cabinet1)
        (inReceptacle soap1 sink1)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle cloth1 cabinet1)
        (isClean cloth1)
        (opened cabinet1)
    ))
)

```pddl
(define (problem put_rinsed_cloth_in_cabinet)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        sink1 - sink
        cabinet1 - receptacle
        cloth1 - object
        candle1 - object
        spray_bottle1 - object
        soap1 - object
        trash_bin1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 cloth1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 cabinet1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (isClean cloth1))
        (not (opened cabinet1))
        (openable cabinet1)
        (inReceptacle soap1 sink1)
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle cloth1 cabinet1)
        (isClean cloth1)
        (opened cabinet1)
    ))
)
```
